Pulsatilla chinensis, also known as the meadow anemone, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. Pulsatilla chinensis is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include isoflavones, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pulsatilla chinensis include dried root and rhizome extracts, which are often used to treat menstrual disorders and other women's health issues.
